events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

Detectives Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde find themselves on the twisting trail of a mysterious reptile who arrives in Zootopia and turns the mammal metropolis upside down. To crack the case, Judy and Nick must go undercover to unexpected new parts of town, where their growing partnership is tested like never before. Rated PG. 1hr 48 mins. English Subtitles for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (SDH) when available.

events.title: Puppet Show: The Story of Queen Esther
events.description: Join us for an interactive performance by puppeteer Hugh Phillips of the Fairytale Puppet Theatre as we celebrate Jewish heritage. The story of Queen Esther will be re-told with hand puppets, live music and humour in a fun, interactive performance. Drop-in. Space is limited.

events.title: P.A. Day Movie: Strange World
events.description: Join the legendary Clade family on an epic adventure as they discover a hidden land filled with extraordinary creatures and unexpected dangers. Drop in, no large groups. Please submit your request at least 2 weeks in advance to: tpl.ca/accessibility-events .

All Ages, Drop in.

Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a), 20(c), and 20(d). The information will only be used for event and program registration, delivery and evaluation. Questions about how the library handles your information can be directed to Answerline staff at answerline@tpl

events.title: Arts & Crafts
events.description: Let your creativity loose at Cliffcrest Library with our Summer Wonder Arts & Crafts program! We'll be making different projects every week in July & August. All supplies will be provided and you get to take your creations home! Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 11:00 to 11:30 . Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play from  11:30 to 12:00 .

events.title: EarlyON Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 5 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment. This program is offered in partnership with the Malvern Family Resource Centre (MFRC) . This is a drop-in program. No registration required.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers.  Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00am. Babies, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play until 11:30am.

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.

events.title: Summer Carnival
events.description: Celebrate the close of Summer Wonder with a carnival filled with games, activities, and lots of fun! Ages 4-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Summer Party
events.description: Join us for a summer party with games, treats, and prizes! Weather permitting, we will celebrate outdoors.  For children of all ages. Drop-in. Light snacks will be served at this program. Please advise staff if your child has any allergies.

events.title: BINGO
events.description: Join us for a bingo session with multiple rounds and prizes. Ages 4-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

events.title: "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" with Author Catherine Little
events.description: On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​ Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book " Jane and the Blue Willow Princess " about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.&

The epic adventure follows the journey of a robot, Rozzum unit 7134, 'Roz' for short, that is shipwrecked on an uninhabited island and must learn to adapt to the harsh surroundings, gradually building relationships with the animals on the island and becoming the adoptive parent of an orphaned gosling. Age Rating: PG Run Time: 1h 42m For Families.

I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

June 1 - Ozobots 1 ** June 8 - Ozobots 2 ** June 15 - Ozobots 3 ** June 22 - Ozobots 4 ** **Please register for Ozobots via the links above. 12 spots per week. Registration begins May 20 at 9:30am.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.
